name: Paystack Transaction Initialization
primary_area: Payments / Checkout / Sales / Ash Actions / Security / Tests
forbidden_changes: Paystack HTTP calls inside Ash resource actions

red_requires_failure_when: Valid active checkout cannot initialize a Paystack transaction
Missing buyer_email calls Paystack without approved policy
Amount/currency/provider_reference can be supplied by client input
Double-submit creates duplicate PaymentAttempts or duplicate provider calls
Provider timeout raises raw exception or marks order paid
PaymentEvent persistence or webhook controller appears in this slice
Ticket issuance, Attendee creation, scanner, WhatsApp, or Redis inventory mutation appears
authorization_url, access_code, raw response, email, phone, Authorization header, or secrets appear in logs
green_requires: Valid active checkout initializes Paystack and creates an idempotent PaymentAttempt
Invalid states return safe errors and do not call Paystack
Duplicate initialization returns existing active attempt/link
Provider failures are normalized and do not mark order paid or consume inventory
StateTransition audit is appended for all state changes
Boundary tests prove no webhook, verification, ticketing, scanner, Redis, or WhatsApp behavior was added
Logs and errors redact all sensitive fields

security_impact: Introduces backend payment-link creation and storage of restricted authorization_url/access_code fields; requires strict redaction and actor checks.
performance_impact: Adds bounded external HTTP call from a service layer with idempotency; must not hold DB transactions across provider calls or touch hot inventory mutation.
